escalation.rationale: Escalation remains at peak High as US-Iran war continues with Iran stepping up missile/drone attacks reaching Bahrain and Jordan, Houthis declare maritime blockade of Saudi ports threatening Bab al-Mandeb and Red Sea shipping, Brent surpasses $94-95/barrel, and Ukraine undergoes sweeping military leadership shakeup while intensifying long-range strikes deep into Russia.

ticker_items: Iran steps up attacks; sirens in Bahrain and Jordan intercept missile/drone waves.
Houthis declare Saudi port blockade; Bab al-Mandeb transits falling sharply.
Zelenskyy ousts Chief of General Staff, one day after replacing top commander Syrsky.
Brent crude surges past $95/barrel on Middle East shipping fears.
US-Ukraine sign drone cooperation agreement; Ukrainian combat drones to be exported for US testing.

eastern_europe.description: Ukraine launched approximately 400 drones at the Moscow region overnight, wounding two people and setting several facilities ablaze.
Ukrainian military struck Wildberries retailer warehouses in the third such attack in a week, part of Kyiv's expanding long-range retaliatory strike campaign.
Ukraine employed FP-5 'Flamingo' missiles with salvaged engines to strike targets deep inside Russian territory for the first time.

middle_east.description: Iran launched missiles and drones in a stepped-up attack wave; Bahrain sirens sounded as two waves were intercepted.
Jordan intercepted Iranian missile and drone waves as Iran escalated its regional attack campaign.
Houthi rebels declared a maritime blockade of Saudi ports and threatened shipping transiting Bab al-Mandeb, causing ships to divert.
US and Israeli attacks on Iran continued as part of the ongoing war, with ceasefire talks ongoing in Pakistan.

today.kia: ~3,468 in Iran; ~3,696 in Lebanon; ~28 in Gulf states

claimed_totals.figure: est. 1,600 casualties in single day (Fark thread claim)

russia.text: Sumy and Kharkiv border areas will not be returned in any peace deal.
russia.speaker: Kremlin spokesperson
russia.title: Kremlin

russia.text: Russia thanks Pyongyang for supporting its war against Ukraine.
russia.speaker: Vladimir Putin
russia.title: President of Russia

ukraine.text: I have been tasked with stepping up counteroffensive actions.
ukraine.speaker: Mykhailo Drapatyi
ukraine.title: Commander-in-Chief, Ukrainian Armed Forces

middle_east.text: Iran is the troublemaker of the Gulf region and any Houthi attempt to block Red Sea shipping would be problematic.
middle_east.speaker: Marco Rubio
middle_east.title: US Secretary of State

middle_east.text: Lebanon is working towards a complete Israeli withdrawal from areas it occupies.
middle_east.speaker: Nawaf Salam
middle_east.title: Prime Minister of Lebanon

middle_east.text: I narrowly escaped the Israeli assassination strike at the opening of the war.
middle_east.speaker: Abbas Araghchi
middle_east.title: Iranian Foreign Minister

middle_east.text: The conflict is now a full-scale war.
middle_east.speaker: Iranian government
middle_east.title: Tehran official statement

observers.description: North Korea reaffirmed support for Russia's war in Ukraine as foreign ministers met in Moscow. Speculation mounts over a possible Kim Jong Un visit to the Kremlin. Pyongyang continues to supply materiel and personnel to Russian forces.

The US is actively engaged in war against Iran and continues to support Ukraine with military aid. Defense Secretary Hegseth has requested additional congressional funding for the Iran war. The US and Ukraine also signed a landmark drone cooperation agreement.

Germany and France are reported to be conducting their first joint nuclear drills described as strategic coordination, in a move interpreted as directed against Russia. Germany continues broader European defense buildup.
France joined Germany in joint nuclear drills as part of European strategic coordination. France continues to support Ukraine diplomatically and militarily within EU frameworks.

Lebanese President Joseph Aoun met with President Trump at the White House in the first such visit in nearly two decades, seeking US support for Israeli withdrawal. Lebanon and Israel have resumed diplomatic relations under US auspices, though southern populations remain skeptical.

Pakistan is serving as a mediator in the US-Iran conflict, hosting talks between a senior Iranian official and other parties aimed at reviving a collapsed ceasefire. Pakistan's role as mediator is a significant diplomatic development.

Saudi Arabia faces a direct Houthi maritime blockade of its ports, adding to its exposure from the broader US-Iran war. Riyadh is navigating extreme pressure between US partnership and Iranian proxy threats, while Gulf business models are disrupted by the ongoing conflict.

financial.notes: Brent crude surged past $95/barrel as Houthi blockade threats reduced Bab al-Mandeb transits and ~6 million barrels/day of crude to Asia are now at risk, triggering the largest single-day Indian equity market decline in two weeks.

update_log.text: Zelenskyy ousts Chief of General Staff one day after replacing top commander Syrsky, completing sweeping military leadership shakeup.
Houthis declare maritime blockade of Saudi ports; EU naval group raises threat level for Red Sea.
Brent crude exceeds $95/barrel; Indian Sensex falls 715 points on Middle East escalation fears.
US and Ukraine sign bilateral drone cooperation agreement; Ukrainian combat drones to be exported to US for military testing.
Iran steps up attacks; Bahrain and Jordan intercept two waves of missiles and drones.
New Ukrainian top commander Drapatyi says he is tasked with stepping up counteroffensive actions.
Ukraine strikes Wildberries warehouses in Russia for the third time in one week as long-range campaign expands.
Al Jazeera casualty tracker confirms ~3,468 dead in Iran, ~3,696 in Lebanon, ~28 in Gulf states from US-Israel attacks.
Kremlin rules out returning Sumy and Kharkiv border areas in any peace settlement.
Ukraine employed FP-5 Flamingo missiles with salvaged engines to strike deep into Russian territory for the first time.
North Korea reaffirms support for Russia as foreign ministers meet in Moscow; Kim visit to Kremlin speculated.
Ukraine launched ~400 drones at Moscow region overnight, wounding two and damaging several facilities.
